summary: Iteration 3 backend regression after pure refactor (server.py split into routers/*.py). Ran full pytest suite via public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL: 34/34 PASS in 36.71s — identical to iterations 1 & 2. Zero regressions across auth, market, strategies, backtest, risk, trap, paper (single + multi-leg), journal, brokers, dashboard, and WebSocket /api/ws/ticks. Verified cross-router shared helpers: dashboard.py imports compute_positions from paper.py (direct async call, no nested HTTP) and multi-leg uses place_paper_order helper. Kill-switch cross-cutting concern (PUT /api/risk/limits {kill_switch:true} → 423 on /api/paper/order/multi-leg) still enforced. Backend logs confirm all endpoints return expected status codes (200/423 etc., no 404/500 regressions).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now an excellent thin composition layer (~80 lines). Router boundaries are clean and per-domain.
routers/dashboard.py imports compute_positions from routers/paper.py — clean async helper reuse (no internal HTTP round-trip). Good.

routers/paper.py exposes place_paper_order() and compute_positions() as plain async helpers — these should ideally be moved to a services/ module rather than living in the router file, so routers stay HTTP-only. Minor architectural nit; not blocking.

WebSocket router is mounted twice (with and without /api prefix) — works, but could be simplified to a single mount under /api since ingress maps /api/* to backend; the bare path is only useful for direct-to-8001 access during local dev.
Lifecycle uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'). Migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager when convenient (cosmetic in current Starlette versions).

All previously flagged review comments from iteration_2 still apply (ENCRYPTION_KEY hard-required at import, ws_feed module-level state, multi-leg partial-failure non-atomicity, /brokers/test returns 200 on status='error', MultiLegLeg.side not enum-validated).
seed_data_creation: Test suite (re-run unchanged) creates TEST_ users via register, broker_connections for zerodha/rmoney, multi-leg baskets, NIFTY paper positions, and cleans them up where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is clean and behaviour-preserving. server.py now only composes routers — any new endpoint should be added to the appropriate routers/<domain>.py file and registered via api.include_router(...) in server.py. WS still works at wss://<host>/api/ws/ticks. Shared helpers (place_paper_order, compute_positions) live in routers/paper.py — consider extracting to services/ module if codebase grows further.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. Pure-refactor iteration: backend internal organization changed from monolithic server.py to per-domain routers/. All 34 regression tests pass identical to baselines. Test harness, public URL, MongoDB data, and frontend integration paths remain unaffected.
